titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Albert Schiller Collection
titlestmt.author: Chris Cole and Adrienne Pruitt

publicationstmt.publisher: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday May 8, 2025 at 19:00

corpname.part: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

did.abstract: This small collection documents Schiller's use of fleurons, or printer's flowers, to make complicated visual imagery. His designs are primarily printed as broadsides.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This small collection documents Schiller's use of fleurons, or printer's flowers, to make complicated visual imagery. His designs are primarily printed as broadsides.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Albert Schiller (1898-1970) was born in Russia and immigrated to the United States in 1904. After leaving high school at the age of 17, Schiller worked in a printshop in Harlem before becoming the type director of the J. Walter Thompson Agency in 1918. From 1924 until 1959, he served as the typographic art director of Advertising Agencies' Service Company, Inc. While there, he produced many typographic compositions and pioneered his innovative "type pictures." To create his type pictures, which often served as the company's Christmas cards for clients, Schiller used printer's ornaments and fleurons to form elaborate scenes of architecture, figures, and decorative borders. After retiring from the Advertising Agencies' Service Company, Schiller established an advertising industry consulting firm, Typographic Laboratory. During his lifetime, his work was exhibited in museums and universities, as well as the Los Angeles Public Library, to which he donated a collection of his type pictures. Schiller died on July 22, 1970 in Brooklyn, New York at the age of 71.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged by size, and then chronologically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Albert Schiller collection, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Titles in quotation marks denote titles taken from the items themselves.
